name: Darling Square Library
address: The Exchange, levels 1 and 2, 1 Little Pier St, Haymarket NSW 2000, Australia
formatted_address: The Exchange, levels 1 and 2, 1 Little Pier St, Haymarket NSW 2000, Australia

reviews.text:
A clean and popular library not far from Paddy's Market Light Rail Stop.

The library is on Level 1 and Level 2, via lift.

The 2 levels have staff assistance desk on each level. Free to join library membership.

A range of seating configurations for reading, studying and laptop use.

Outdoor terrace available for users to duck out to take a phone call or have a sandwich.

Toilet and general environment is clean.

Photocopying, printing and scanning are available. Scanning to USB is free. Refer to photo for printing and copying costs.

Water cooler is available to top up your water bottle.

Recycle chutes available for recycling batteries, blister packs, electronics and light bulbs.

Great place to work/study. Two levels means plenty of desks available. Easy to get to via public transport. Plenty of eating places close-by. Plenty of current magazines to catch up on the gossips. Clean. The free Wi-Fi is good speed to work/study. The limited amount of data is reasonable. I love it the power available at each desk. Would be nicer if there are some pot plants outside the balcony to feel less sterile.

As someone who had to wait for almost 12 hours until they could check in to their hotel, this library was a godsend. Spacious, had lots of quiet little corners to tuck yourself away, plug points to charge, clean of course books to help pass the time. There's lots of books in different languages too. Toilets were nice and clean, it could be more quiet but some people's children don't know how to behave in public and that's not the library's fault. It was air conditioned too! I really appreciate free public spaces like this; I was very relieved to be somewhere that didn't require you to spend money or be outside in the humid heat. Very easy to get to, maybe a bit confusing to find since you'll need to find the elevator to go up.
A fantastic library located in the Exchange Building, offering plenty of seating though often quite full, with the convenience of grabbing food downstairs.

Easily enough floor space for more than triple the seating yet for some reason it's sparser than an art gallery. It seems more like it's built to be pretty than to be a functional centre where people can come to work or study. Would recommend visiting almost any other library as it's too hard to get a seat here.

name: Town Hall Library Express
address: Town Hall House, Level 1/456 Kent St, Sydney NSW 2001, Australia
formatted_address: Town Hall House, Level 1/456 Kent St, Sydney NSW 2001, Australia

reviews.text: Doesn't even have a printer. I feel it really needs to be renamed from a library

It’s quite a small place, and for a library, there are only two large tables. Whenever someone writes at these tables, there’s always a self-proclaimed “justice warrior” who jumps in to tell them not to write too loudly. But honestly, the issue lies with the table itself—not the people using it. It’s unfair to blame others for a structural problem.

Also, the signage is really unclear. It’s very difficult to find where this place actually is, which makes the experience even more confusing for first-time visitors.

It's not a real library. It's just a corner under the Town Hall building where there are some books you can borrow. There are two tables you can use your laptops on and read books. It's next to a Cafe with no door in between, so it's noisy most of the time. There are toilets here too. A good spot to have some rest nonetheless.

It's not a library site but a 'link' which means in practice about four desks, news papers and a small range of books. The desks are well setup for laptop users. It's fairly quiet and it was easy to find a space. Also on the floor is a good, cheap cafe. So a good hideyhole for doing some work.

For the library: small space with access to power and USB ports. Big comfy chairs but the tables near computers are too tall to use for study. Toilets are close by and clean. Wifi and cafe make it a cosy space to take a chill break when in the hustle bustle of the city.

name: Customs House Library
address: 31 Alfred St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia
formatted_address: 31 Alfred St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia

reviews.text: A lovely library right in the heart of the city. It’s modern, clean, and has spots to sit read or study. The staff are friendly and willing to help, and there’s a good selection of books and resources, for children as well. A sweet little girl, about 4-5 years old, even brought me a book from her tiny hands the first time we saw each other.  Such a heart-warming moment... A welcoming place, perfect for a quiet break from the busy city outside.

A handsome heritage building beautifully restored with inside a modern public space for art, library facilities and a rooftop restaurant. Right in front of Sydney Harbour with the Opera House, ferries and excellent transport links.

Small library but has a charming reading room. There’s a room with CDs, sheet music and other audio/music-related materials, which is worth browsing. Visited towards closing hours on a Sunday and it was pretty quiet. A good place to rest and get away from the noise of touristy Circular Quay.

Love this library. It's my favourite library in Sydney. It's huge and the layout is very neat. ( As opposed to Green Square library that's in an odd shape and very packed, with tables and shelves close to each other, organized messily) There are several areas for you if you want a quiet space with fewer people. Computers are available for your use, and printers.
The reason why I can’t rate 6 stars because of five. One of the quietest study environment than Hornsby and Chatswood library. Plus, another 1/5 super stars awarded due to my satisfying experience.

name: Conservatorium Library - The University of Sydney Library
address: 1 Conservatorium Rd, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia
formatted_address: 1 Conservatorium Rd, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia

reviews.text: The best branch of the USYD library and the best library in Sydney. The librarians are always super friendly.
Nice and quiet

name: Kings Cross Library
address: 1/50-52 Darlinghurst Rd, Potts Point NSW 2011, Australia
formatted_address: 1/50-52 Darlinghurst Rd, Potts Point NSW 2011, Australia

reviews.text: A great place to study. Not too crowded and well maintained. They don’t like people playing with their jigsaw puzzle though. If they saw one, they will later take the chair away.

Generally love it here, friendly and helpful staff, reasonable prices for printing, pleasant selection of books. The only thing is the kids section has moved and seemed to have shrunk, which is a pity. Still overall 5 stars for being just great!

How does opening library at 10am makes any sense?
Imo. Library should be open from 8am to 10pm
Users that provide benefits to society, & whose tax $ pays library bills need the service before or after work - in our techie world anything helping startups or promotes digital innovation should be encouraged.

What’s the point of opening it during office hours, unless it’s catering to boomers and students exclusively.

I get staff is lazy but the service is not meant for staff, it’s meant for the public.. it’s easy to employ better staff.
Lots of space to work or read – and friendly staff.

My happy place in the 'hood. Will be even better once the replacement lift is ready. The only problem with the place is the (very) occasional person with issues coming up from the gentrifying strip below but the staff always seem able to cope.

name: Navitas Library
address: level 8/255 Elizabeth St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia
formatted_address: level 8/255 Elizabeth St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia

reviews.text: Very small library. Also you can't lock your computer so if you've got hours of research or any confidential documents up you'll have to close it all just to use the bathroom. The only options allowed is to log off completely or shut down the computer. And finally, the library isn't open on weekends. It's best to go elsewhere.
Wish it would stay open for longer hours and during weekends!
Excellent quiet place and best for study.
Nice

name: Caroline Simpson Library & Research Collection
address: Level 1/10 Macquarie St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia
formatted_address: Level 1/10 Macquarie St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia

reviews.text: The Caroline Simpson Library & Research Collection is housed in one of the oldest surviving public buildings in Sydney. The renovation of the site was beautifully designed and carried out. Drop in for a short visit, even if you're not a researcher. Wander quietly around the building if only to soak in the work that went into the renovation.

I love this library. Lovely ambience to do your research. As long as you're patient and methodical, you will find something...even if you didn't know you were looking for it! And when you're stuck, the staff are amazingly resourceful and helpful. I shall return!

Treasure trove of beauty and interesting stories, it’s wonderful. Staffed by people who care and are very welcoming. Outside there’s a gorgeous courtyard garden.

This library and museum is a national treasure in my view.

Saw the other reviews. I have been using this library for years when I have the time. It is a small quiet, old style library. Yes it has glass but hardly anyone is looking at you and if you are doing research, you won't notice anyway. The staff are very, very helpful and friendly. Because you can't borrow the books and magazines it means what you are looking for will most probably be available. I've found it to be an oasis of peace and quiet in the bustling CBD. It is what I call one of my happy places! It is a library for books rather than technology.

Designed like a fish bowl - so everyone working at the Sydney Living Museum HQ can peer at you through the glass - as you try to focus on your research. This is naturally disquieting. Staff are only as friendly as they need to be. No borrowing here - as is the case with the place up the road. No one trusts anyone anymore. No WiFi.

name: Surry Hills Library
address: 405 Crown St, Surry Hills NSW 2010, Australia
formatted_address: 405 Crown St, Surry Hills NSW 2010, Australia

reviews.text: I try and study here and have the same problems as everyone else - people should be quiet in a library and there should not be a kids area if the design of the building magnifies any noise from this area and parents don't keep their kids quiet. It would be great if you could repurpose the kids space as study space is so severely lacking, but reviews from many years ago call for the same thing and clearly nothing has been done. Staff are lovely, building looks idyllic from the outside.

A great spot for those that enjoy studying while people watching!
However, I'd recommend against coming if you prefer quiet study spot or are planning to study for extended periods of time.

Make sure to get there when they open as seating is highly limited.

Bloody good place to go! 🌸
Kate’s super beautiful 💕🌟 & sooo nice 🥰
Super supportive and helpful 🙌✨
She really helped me out heaps 🙏💛
Big big thanks to Kate! 🫶😘💌✨

#SweetService #LovelyKate #GratefulHeart #BloodyGood #FeelGoodVibes

I grew up in Surry Hills and the library but it wasn't until recently when i started needing a place to study that i noticed how terrible this place is. There are really only two main places to sit the front of the library on the main floor and near the staircase on the second floor these two dedicated places only sit about 14-18 people in total! It's impossible to find a place to sit most of the time and even if you do find a place to sit there are about four people or wandering around at all time looking to snatch up a seat when it becomes free. The staff are highly uninterested and couldn't even muster up a welcoming smile, for such a big place they haven't used their space to the best of their ability. Even if there were a few more chairs that would help but clearly this librarys interests aren't in a good use of space.

Pretty nice library but there are definitely better options out there. It is always packed no matter what day it is and has minimal seating spots and tables for those pooking to do work on their laptops or to write some notes. Selection of books are decent, though it does not have everything as i was unable to find a specific book.

name: Balmain Library
address: Rear of/370 Darling St, Balmain NSW 2041, Australia
formatted_address: Rear of/370 Darling St, Balmain NSW 2041, Australia

reviews.text: The library has a good range of books and a nice children’s section, but it can get quite noisy and crowded at times, which makes it hard to focus. It’s still a useful community space, but not always the best spot if you’re looking for quiet reading or study.
Friendly staff and quiet library for study. Great range of books.
Well presented and functional library. Great staff and spaces for study
My daughter loved the school holiday activities offered here. Thankyou

The renovations at Balmain Library are stunning but since it reopened, the air conditioning is too cold. I understand that summer is coming and the outside temperatures are rising, but inside it feels like a freezer. Because of this, I often think about whether to use the facilities, because once I arrive, the cold makes me feel like I have to run out. It’s a nice space, but it’s uncomfortable to work in with the AC so high.

name: TAFE Ultimo Library
address: Mary Ann St, Ultimo NSW 2007, Australia
formatted_address: Mary Ann St, Ultimo NSW 2007, Australia

reviews.text: The TAFE library is such a welcoming and inviting space. The staff are always friendly and happy to help, and the environment makes it easy to focus and get work done. There are plenty of computers available, which is super convenient, especially during busy times. It’s a great place to study or just take a quiet break between classes.
Susan was amazing helping me with my issues. Everything worked great!
Great librarians, I wish students would also keep the break room/lounge clean.
A large library on three floors, with a lot of different books, a quiet space for studying, many computers for research and places for group study.  Next to the student association section.
Very good 👍

name: Ultimo Library
address: Level 1, Ultimo Community Centre, 40 William Henry St, Ultimo NSW 2007, Australia
formatted_address: Level 1, Ultimo Community Centre, 40 William Henry St, Ultimo NSW 2007, Australia

reviews.text: Very comfortable library, I am an odd looking fella and maybe coz of that I've been snubbed by some staff at other libraries, well the Ultimo Library Staff have been so friendly and welcoming I was surprised how friendly they are. I don't normally leave reviews especially positive ones, this positive review was much deserved ☯️

Big shout out to Mia at Ultimo and Darling Square libraries! She really went above and beyond to introduce us to all the services that the library has to offer that I otherwise would never have known about. Her positive and friendly attitude made us feel so welcome. Thank you Mia for making our day, you’re a gem! ⭐️
So good place to do my assessments

my local hang to read the Sunday papers for free, a nice comfortable cool/warm place to relax on a too hot/cold/rainy day.

Others complain about kids crying but it is a community centre, not just for overseas students wanting quiet with their free internet.

Old Chinese folk love the free Chinese newspapers and Chinese books here also for kids, and there is a kids play area where untrained kids tend to make too much noise sometimes - until they are told.  A good community centre.

Also free computers with free access to Ancestry for family history research.

One of the worst libraries in the inner city. It's basically a sport complex with a section of books. This library has its priorities way out of whack. There is basically no where to sit and study - especially if you need a power outlet. They have prioritised a large staff area and book shelves over usable space. Most people don't borrow books any more but there is still a huge need for school and uni students, jobseekers and those who need a break from WFH to have good spaces with power.

name: Pyrmont Library Link
address: 79A John St, Pyrmont NSW 2009, Australia
formatted_address: 79A John St, Pyrmont NSW 2009, Australia

reviews.text: This library link is not staffed, but you can pick up reserves and return books here, as well as sit in the couches and read, or borrow from the free community book shelves
Great environment! Really helpful

I went to the library, which was very close to where I live. It was small, but the person there was very friendly. He also showed me a space where I could get a free book, tea, or coffee. I told him I wanted an Australian novel for my daughter, something about overcoming adversity or a mystery novel. He hesitated for a while and then asked for 15 copies. Fifteen minutes later, I was impressed. On a piece of A4 paper, the front side was written in English about overcoming adversity, with the mystery novel's translation in Korean underneath. Another piece of A4 paper had the name and address of a nearby bookstore. I was so grateful that I offered him some Korean candy I had in my bag, along with a chewy fruit candy from Korea. I was so grateful. While the library seems like a place to pick up and return books, it was a great place to stop by, with comfortable sofas and warm coffee and tea. The only thing I wish there were was two desks and two chairs. I wanted to go there to study.

name: Chatswood Library
address: Lower Ground, The Concourse Chatswood, 409 Victoria Ave, Chatswood NSW 2067, Australia
formatted_address: Lower Ground, The Concourse Chatswood, 409 Victoria Ave, Chatswood NSW 2067, Australia

reviews.text: Is humongous the right word to describe the sheer size of this library?  The amenities are great with a variety of seating spaces and configurations at different areas of the library.  They have various small conference and study rooms available for hire or special complimentary use, plus regular workshops on a variety of topics and genre.  I’ve attended quite a number like art and craft, group activities and talks.  I organised a small successful art exhibition in one of the rooms.  Most of the staff are friendly and helpful.  Really good for study, research and just relaxed reading, although some young groups couldn’t keep their voices down.  Hopefully the staff would remind them it’s still a library and not a chat room.
It appears that half of the library building extends below ground level.

One of the best libraries in Sydney. The architect did a great job bringing in the natural light to the library in the basement level and added the water feature to soften the concrete modern building. In the evening the library was full of high school students studying and there was some photography lighting class going on. This library looked like it is being used to its fullest potential when others are closing down in this time and age of digital era.

Great library,  having a lot of  various types of books, spacious space for study and reading. There is a zone for children

However, due to covid19, their opening is irregular. You must check trading hours before a visit.

Good library but limited seating, just have to get here early. However, I think it would be really beneficial to get new lower seating on the higher tables because the table is too low, making it really uncomfortable to get work done.

name: Ocean Life Street Library
address: Active Kids Homebush, 40 Hornsey Rd, Homebush West NSW 2140, Australia
formatted_address: Active Kids Homebush, 40 Hornsey Rd, Homebush West NSW 2140, Australia

reviews.text: This is not a library. But a small local street self bookshelf. Will attach a photo, didn't have to travel 15minutes by drive.
Street Library managed by Strathfield Council library services..

name: Lidcombe Library
address: 3 Bridge St, Lidcombe NSW 2141, Australia
formatted_address: 3 Bridge St, Lidcombe NSW 2141, Australia

reviews.text:
Don't let the outside put you off, a lovely open space inside. Some individual study spots, and some spots for group discussion too (I asked if I could work on something with a friend here and they said that's totally fine, as long as we weren't too loud)

Very friendly librarians too.
Really nice library, just wish it had extended hours offered

Doesn’t have a wide selection of books but the librarians are nice enough.Air in the library smells musty and like a dump.Maybe it was just when I went but  I’m informing you in advance.Isn’t very loud unlike some libraries but the opening times are not very long.
Conveniently located library with friendly staff, small albeit decent collection of books which do appear to be updated frequently. Sad that the opening hours are limited and not open on Sundays.
Library location is conveniently near the station...staff is helpful...not noisy...needs extended hours

name: Strathfield Council Library & Innovation Hub
address: 65-67 Rochester St, Homebush NSW 2140, Australia
formatted_address: 65-67 Rochester St, Homebush NSW 2140, Australia

reviews.text:
Pros:
Big library. Staffs are supportive & well informed. Well organised library. Prints and book leading facilities are automated and easy. Location is very close to Homebush station which is really nice. Excellent collection of books for people of all ages.

Cons:
Nothing

I come here often. The staff are amazing, the extended hours are amazing, the low cost vending machine is amazing, the community who visit are amazing and the actual space and building and layout and the fact that this is nestled in the middle of a (mostly) low density residential suburb makes this one of the best libraries in all of Sydney for me.

Place is clean but very loud. It's not random people, it's not angry baby. It's the librarians. Yup the very people who are suppose to be the one telling you off are speaking in high volumes as if I am forced to hear their conversation. Also there's someone who types on the keyboard at the computer area at 2nd floor really loud. He types like as if he is stress testing the product. This place can get wild.

Went here only few days ago for the first time. I really like the library. Really great, spacious, very clean. Well maintained all the cleaning regulations. Lot's of great books, I found most of stuff I been looking for.  And most importantly the library staff is very friendly and helpful. Thanks for that!
Super kind staff! Was able to get a card without hassle and they are super nice. The place is also amazing with a lot of chairs, computers, and book selections.

name: Burwood Library
address: 2 Conder St, Burwood NSW 2134, Australia
formatted_address: 2 Conder St, Burwood NSW 2134, Australia

reviews.text: The burwood  library was my very first time there, even though I was having a good shady relaxing time, having morning tea outside the library. The inside part was very air conditioning with people reading their books whilst I read a Marvel book for a minimum time. They were toilets upstairs and downstairs as well, which only one person could go inside downstairs. It was very satisfying that there was a fill-up water bottle, too. The best library with not a lot of chairs thought. I really enjoyed my time with a friend of mine.

Asian lady by name of Sabrina absolutely disgusting service. check revies of reference to "Asian lady" shor back hair approximately 160cm tall. She was extremely rude and aggresive trying to kick me out of the library at 3.50pm when it closes at 4pm. She stood not far away from me watching me then started to tell and harrass me to pack up and leave now because then the door eill be locked and I wouldnt be let out. it eas still only 3.55
I told her to stop that I was aware of the tume and eill leave before 4 but she continued like some sort of police officer!!!  this is a COMMUNITY LIBRARY and she should not be working in such a place if she cannot be respectful and kind of others. i was simply trying to finsg something and I was still out the door before 4pm. Absolutely disgusting and I seee many many complaints about her inclsuing her beinh racist which makes me question if she was being racists towards me and hence the hostile treatmne.t i will be making a foemal complaint about her. the rest of the staff are always nice and understanding even the security guard (which she seems to have some confusion that is what she is) made some nice suggestions about using the litbary wifi outside the library so I could continue my work. she simply was determind to kick me out like I was an animal..absolute disgraceful!!

I was able to start my paper work and concentrate because of the very nice ambience in Burwood Library. 5 stars as it's the best library I have ever been to. Very clean toilet and comfy lounge areas. You can have your own peaceful space. Few good things here is that they have USB ports to charge your gadgets, and you can print documents. You're gonna need a card though--not sure yet how to get it. I will definitely go back to this library. I hope they can extend until 6pm.
I visited for the first time today, and this library is one of the prettiest I’ve visited in Sydney! Lots of great space to study and really colourful, very clear signage and comfy seating areas.

This is my first time studying here, and I like this cosy little space.

When I got here, I was happily surprised that there was a pop up coffee shop right out of this library. I am willing to give a five star review for any libraries if there is a coffee shop 2m away lol.

Also, they have a rainbow sticker on the glass gate, which is another reason why I am happy to give a 5 star review.

I have posted 4 photos, and you can see they do put LGBTQIA+ contents out, and I really appreciate that they have a unisex bathroom (and another unisex/disabled bathroom). As a trans femme person, I don’t feel comfy using male/female bathrooms, and I would like to give a 10 star review for the bathrooms lol.

There are two main study areas as I have discovered, correct me if I was wrong. As per my posted photo, you can see they have comfy seats and USB charging decks for each seat, which is great for long studying sessions.

Overall, I am very happy with my time spent here.

name: ACU Strathfield Library
address: 25A Barker Rd, Strathfield NSW 2135, Australia
formatted_address: 25A Barker Rd, Strathfield NSW 2135, Australia

reviews.text: Very helpful IT staff but the it would be better if staff worked earlier hours.
Friendly staff members of ACU when I came today for a trip from Glenwood High School. 👍
Great location, soccer fields are well kept, one  grass field and one turf.
good place to work online and study; most books are up to date :)
Good library resources, ample of space to sit around and study.

name: Regents Park Library
address: 1 Amy St, Regents Park NSW 2143, Australia
formatted_address: 1 Amy St, Regents Park NSW 2143, Australia

reviews.text: Nice little place for some reading/study. However the single toilet doesn’t have any toilet paper. Also some people inside the library are loud/on the phone and library staff don’t seem to care. Would be really nice if the librarian could enforce a no-phone or no-noise policy so others can enjoy the facilities in peace

The librarians are okay but the library itself is amazing. It has a multitude of books to choose from and provides services such as internet, printers, devices and programs. The library also provides access to a few private rooms and access to be able to reserve books from other Cumberland Council libraries.
Note: You might catch the librarians on a bad day.

Overall a very poor experience at this library. There are no power-points anyone can use to charge their devices or laptops while studying (except behind the couches), so you are 'required to come to the library with fully charged laptops' if you wish to use them for any work. Most librarians don't make any attempt to help you and make you feel as though you are unwanted in the library. The library was not designed for students or for the 21st century and is in dire need of some attention.

It is very clean, organised and accessible, just a minute walk from train station. While the building is not as big as Parramatta Library, it can cater your need. The PCs are good with fast Internet and nice printer - HP multi function. The staff is friendly, I only ask when I need something. I am lucky because I am very computer literated, I rarely have problem while sitting, reading or using the PCs.
Not a great variety of books,the librarians aren’t very friendly,loud and there is only one toilet.The library is clean but there are many problems with it.

name: Veech Library
address: 99 Albert Rd, Strathfield NSW 2135, Australia
formatted_address: 99 Albert Rd, Strathfield NSW 2135, Australia

reviews.text: Why not for public
Private access only. Not open for public 😅
Excellent range of books.

name: Wentworth Point Community Centre and Library
address: 10 Footbridge Bvd, Wentworth Point NSW 2127, Australia
formatted_address: 10 Footbridge Bvd, Wentworth Point NSW 2127, Australia

reviews.text:
I had a disappointing customer service experience at Wentworth Point Library. I asked a staff member whether my library card had expired because I couldn’t access after-hours. Instead of explaining clearly, she told me to scan a QR code to book an induction session and “read about it”  - and the QR code she directed me to appeared to be the wrong one. When I asked what the induction session is and how often it needs renewal (as I could access last year), she didn’t provide a clear answer and seemed more focused on her screen than helping.

Another staff member assisted me shortly after and resolved it quickly and kindly, which highlighted the difference in service.

Nicole also overheard my conversation with the other staff member and said she had “already explained,” but it wasn’t clear to me. When I said there shouldn’t be an issue with repeating information if a customer doesn’t understand, she replied that I “could have asked” her to repeat it - I felt I was asking for clarification.

Overall, the attitude felt dismissive and unhelpful. I hope the library provides staff coaching around clearer communication and respectful service. I even emailed this in January but didn’t still hear back.

The staff is very friendly and helpful. The weekly yoga and painting classes have been amazing for me. They are great value for money, help make good friends and really great to help with creating a good headspace after a rough week at work. Look forward to rejoining them next semester! Keep up the good work!

I think it is one of the best libraries and community centers I have ever been in Sydney. The after hours section is quite convenient. It has a friendly staff and it is a great place for everyone. Thanks for your hard work.

This one female staff member has the nastiest attitude and does not deserve to work at a community centre with the way she treats people. Always kicking people out when they're trying to study, even though it's a library. Instead of being polite, she is rude and cocky. There is also a countless number of children screaming and running around. I understand that they are children, but shouldn't the parents have control of them and at least try to have respect for those studying?

The library is run as more of a local community centre as opposed to a library. The meeting rooms are located right next to the study areas and are hired out for loud activities such as dance classes and martial arts. The activities bring a lot of children and as a result there is yelling, screaming and running around the study areas. The noise and chaos of upstairs results in the quiet study room being full all opening hours along with tables downstairs.

The library staff do their best to deal with disruptive behaviour but the library is so poorly laid out it’s a losing battle

name: Dee Why Library
address: 725 Pittwater Rd, Dee Why NSW 2099, Australia
formatted_address: 725 Pittwater Rd, Dee Why NSW 2099, Australia

reviews.text: The book chat sessions are lovely led by wonderful Ms. Sarah. Amazing little community of book chatters held every month first Friday 2-3pm. The library is neat and clean with cute green flowing plants. The only off putting thing is noise level sometimes which are due to ill mannered children and school teens  who are super loud and interfere with the focus.

The books are in an awesome condition. Library is clean and staff is very friendly.
I love spending my time here. Thanks to sarah who takes the book chat sessions - my reading has become 10x.
I have deducted 1 star for noise - adults are having telephone video calls ( a woman is always there on her fixed spot) and children are recklessly shouting all over without any parent’s intervention.
Marcus was very supportive with superb customer service skills.
Great library, peaceful when there isn't a room full of kids making loud noises, private booths, large space. Big carpark. Staff very friendly

It could be a great place, good number of computers, has potential but as far as a library should be minimum quiet, this one is far from that. Mornings are busy with kids plying around and afternoon full with teenagers pretending they are going to study when actually just catching up after school to gossip what’s happening at school and their social lives. Great for flirting if you are their age, as that’s all they come here to do.

name: Campbelltown Library, HJ Daley
address: Downtown Plaza, Shop 2.01/147-157 Queen St, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
formatted_address: Downtown Plaza, Shop 2.01/147-157 Queen St, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia

reviews.text: Great space for study, reading,  and quiet time. There are both stairs and an elevator between floors. There are public computers, study areas, photocopiers, and toilets. You can get a membership to borrow books or use printers. Great resources, staff were happy to help and assist.  Outside parking lot can get very busy with nearby up to 2 hour parking spots.

I just visited the newly relocated Campbelltown HJ Daley Library in the Old Spotlight building on Queen Street.

The Creative Studio is my favourite feature of the library. It boasts everything from a 3D printer, green screen, audio recorders, scanners, cameras, to sewing machines, and it's got PCs loaded with Adobe Creative Suite, Blender, and Audacity. There’s also a Wacom graphics tablet, lighting & tripods, microphones, and even a brand new laser cutting machine, basically everything you need to bring your creative ideas to life. Can't wait to try some of the equipment out.

The space is thoughtfully designed, with both stairs and an elevator making it fully accessible between floors. You'll find public computers, study areas, photocopiers, and toilets on both levels, ideal for productivity and comfort. Plus, the meeting rooms and quiet study areas are a lovely touch if you need space for group work or focused reading.

A heads up about parking: the car park offers both 2 hour and 4 hour zones, be sure to double check where you’ve parked to avoid a fine or a rushed return.

Visited the newly relocated Campbelltown library. It has now moved to Downtown Plaza on Queen street.
The library is now on two levels and looks nice as newly designed and colour looks good.
It has meeting rooms, quiet zones for study etc.
The best part about the library is its location. Now it is very near to Campbelltown train and bus stations, which means easily accessible using public transport. Now you can issue a book while travelling back from office.
The Parking is also quite good with good parking space.
Hopefully the library now would have new and more books added to its stock.
Also more events or activities would be organised for community people.
Thanks

I would like to express my sincere appreciation for Julie, who works at the Campbelltown Library. She was incredibly helpful during my visit and went above and beyond to provide outstanding service. From the moment I approached her, Julie was welcoming, patient, and genuinely committed to assisting me.

I needed support with scanning and preparing several documents, and Julie guided me through every step of the process. She didn’t just point me in the right direction—she actively assisted, explained each part clearly, and ensured everything was done correctly. Her professionalism, attention to detail, and friendly approach made what could have been a stressful experience feel easy and well-supported.

It’s rare to come across staff who show this level of dedication and care. Julie truly exemplifies excellent customer service, and Campbelltown Library is lucky to have her. I’m extremely grateful for her help and highly commend her for her exceptional assistance.

Not enough places have unnecessarily long names...
Anyway, spacious and very quiet with many well set-out intriguing biographies of Australians I have never heard of; these caught my eye, pleased me, and reminded me why I shall be back.
I even joined up at the world's smallest manned desk.

name: Ingleburn Library, Greg Percival
address: Cumberland Rd &, Oxford Rd, Ingleburn NSW 2565, Australia
formatted_address: Cumberland Rd &, Oxford Rd, Ingleburn NSW 2565, Australia

reviews.text:
Was a positive experience. Somewhat modern and a credit to the area. There a no inductions or tour of the library when you sign up. Staff were helpful.

One area of improvement is that the community notice board is in the toilet area. As soon as you walk past you get a sewage smell which hits you..
I'm yet to try the ablution facility but going for what I can smell..it might not be pleasant.

STUDENTS BEWARE — DO NOT TRUST THESE “STUDY ROOMS”
This place is an absolute joke. The so-called study rooms aren’t private rooms at all, staff constantly peer in and give judgemental, disgusted looks that make it impossible to concentrate. The rules are a circus: their sign says “no moving furniture,” but whiteboards are shuffled between rooms all the time. When my group moved a whiteboard back to use it, a staff member rudely told us off even though I seen the same whiteboard other rooms days earlier.

Bookings give no equipment list, so you never know what you’ll get, I walked into Room 2 and there was one chair. ONE. For a room that’s meant for study groups. That’s incompetent and unfair. Staff are hostile, enforcement is random, and the whole system feels designed to frustrate students, not help them.

If you’re an HSC or uni student trying to actually study, save yourself the stress, find another place. This library’s “rooms” are unreliable, poorly run, and staffed by people who act like policing students is their favourite pastime.

A wheelchair accessible library named after Harold Gregory (Greg) Percival a former Mayor of Campbelltown. The library is located centrally in the Ingleburn CBD and conveniently near bus and train routes. From humble beginnings in a former Ingleburn RSL building to its current modern incarnation, opening on the 7th March 2009. A very disability friendly library 📚

I visit this library every fortnight with my daughters. The library is well maintained and the staffs are keeping a high Standard service thru out. My family always feels comfortable and inclusive every time we visit. Highly recommended.
Enthusiastic and helpful librarian. A great place to invest your time to improve your knowledge

name: Panania Library and Knowledge Centre
address: Cnr Tower Street &, Anderson Ave, Panania NSW 2213, Australia
formatted_address: Cnr Tower Street &, Anderson Ave, Panania NSW 2213, Australia

reviews.text: Lovely little library, kind hearted staff, I'm a funny looking fellow and sometimes I get snobbed or slightly mistreated simply because of my looks, but the Panania Library staff have made me feel very welcome. Very professional and overall good atmosphere relaxing library ☯️

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Panania Library is such a helpful and well-equipped community space. Most of the facilities are on self-service scan mode, which makes things quick and easy. I needed a couple of items printed, and the lady at the counter was so kind and assisted me on two occasions — really appreciated her help! A great local library with friendly staff and excellent facilities. 📚🖨️✨

I've been coming to this library since I was 5, and it has always been an amazing experience. The library is always clean and peaceful, and the librarians (especially Veronica) have been nothing but kind and extremely helpful, always going out of their way for the customers. Thank you so much for everything!!
Spacious library. Usually parking but competitive. Online book reserve and easy collection. Not sure why they are rebuilding. Padstow library really needs the make over.

I like this library. It's such a fun and relaxing vibe. The people who work there are always so sweet, patient and kind. Their range of books isn't too large, but they have so many more books available online to reserve, and they always come in so quick. I love it here

name: Manly Library
address: 1 Market Ln, Manly NSW 2095, Australia
formatted_address: 1 Market Ln, Manly NSW 2095, Australia

reviews.text:
A free service provided by the council which is awesome. The staff is very nice. Many services are offered. The app is very useful and makes your life easier to renew, pre book or find a book. You can find books but also magazines, audio book, CD and DVD.
New books are quite sparse and not many best sellers.
This is the great, place I were. You have books for learning and something non fiction for fun. Well some books weren't there. But still. This library is the best.
Well located, small and friendly. A worthwhile place if you're also visiting the Manly market, a few meters away.
Manly Library is a great spot to study or read, with plenty of quiet spaces and a welcoming atmosphere. I’m really pleased with my membership so far.

This library offers great services to the local community, functioning as a veritable community space for interactions and meetings. the library has great spaces to read and the staff are always there to provide helpful support and advice.

name: Public Bike Pump and Maintenence Station
address: Manly NSW 2095, Australia
formatted_address: Manly NSW 2095, Australia

reviews.text: Loving Samsung and Google sorry Apple i want my soul back lol

name: Harbord Literary Institute
address: 31 Lawrence St, Freshwater NSW 2096, Australia
formatted_address: 31 Lawrence St, Freshwater NSW 2096, Australia

reviews.text: Had my daughters 1st birthday here. The park area out the back is quite good
The smallest library but priceless to locally borrow a book to read for adults and children. Plenty of picture books for children.
Phony scandals and Machiavellian plots aplenty at monthly Harbord ALP branch meeting. Join today!
Great place for kids parties!
best party venue

name: Christian Science Reading Room
address: 26 Howard Ave, Dee Why NSW 2099, Australia
formatted_address: 26 Howard Ave, Dee Why NSW 2099, Australia

reviews.text: I really like and I recommend this church ❤️

name: Mona Vale Library
address: 1 Park St, Mona Vale NSW 2103, Australia
formatted_address: 1 Park St, Mona Vale NSW 2103, Australia

reviews.text:
A quality and compact collection of books in Mona Vale. Excellent customer service received from the staff named John!
Highly recommended.

Lovely and friendly staff make going to this library a treat. For older people like myself they have the daily newspapers, and of course they have a small outdoor area as well, for a bit of fresh air. Highly recommended for anyone who likes to read in a quiet space.
Love the library as a library, not so much when it becomes a Kindergarten

Lovely quiet atmosphere, great study space. The library has a good selection of books across all topics, as well as music, movies and other media types.

The staff at Mona Vale Library are so friendly, relaxed and welcoming. They give you space and are always available to help out.

Very busy local library, when I was there almost all the reading and desk spaces were in use. Lots of children using the play spaces. Well staffed, good computer access. Visited again today and the space is much less busy,  Year 12 students no longer studying for exams. Spotted a Tech help desk while there. Lots of help with a research project from very helpful staff.

name: Coolangatta Street Library
address: 13 Iluka Ave, Elanora Heights NSW 2101, Australia
formatted_address: 13 Iluka Ave, Elanora Heights NSW 2101, Australia

name: Avalon Community Library
address: 59A Old Barrenjoey Rd, Avalon Beach NSW 2107, Australia
formatted_address: 59A Old Barrenjoey Rd, Avalon Beach NSW 2107, Australia

reviews.text: Such a quiet and calm space in a lovely light and airy building. The app is so user-friendly and has all the latest books. The electronic reminders when your reserves come in are super helpful. The staff are always friendly and have good recommendations.
Lovely local library, friendly and welcoming space. Love it 😊

Lovely community library with super helpful volunteers. Lots of powerpoints at the desks. Lovely breezy place to sit and study or read.

UPDATE: this library is also very cold in winter. No heating and they have ceiling fans on and windows open even on the coldest days with no heating.
Award for rudest librarians I’ve ever met.

Amazing community library, staffed predominantly by volunteers who are so helpful. Nothing is too much trouble for them.
Great range of DVDs, magazines and audio books and there's even a streaming service called Kanopy! + many other e-resources so you can read on your phone or tablet!

name: Five Dock Library
address: Level 1/4/12 Garfield St, Five Dock NSW 2046, Australia
formatted_address: Level 1/4/12 Garfield St, Five Dock NSW 2046, Australia

reviews.text:
Very quiet being school holidays & was a very relaxing and very cool air conditioned place to be on this hot day. And the Librarian was a lovely, very friendly & approachable woman.
As it was quiet  she even quickly vacuumed - the other older staff could learn a thing or two from her.

I visited the library to study, but there was continuous repetitive noise from a child in the main area, which made it very hard to concentrate.
I understand children are welcome, but there should be clearer quiet zones or better noise management for people who come here to work or study. Unfortunately, this disrupted my visit. I hope this can be improved.

Great selection of current and classic books for adults and children, PRC books, Book reservations are filled quickly and even extended if you can’t pick them up within a week from the order shelf.
For a workspace:
quiet work area with WiFi and plugs, printer, helpful staff.
Bathroom facilities have been repaired after request.
WiFi sometimes unstable, so bring a phone to hotspot from.
You can get lunch from nearby Coles or Restaurants.

Great location, facilities, staff and catalogue.
However, there is too much noise for a library.
People talk on their mobile or with other people, which makes concentration hard. Plus school kids come to hang around, so too much talking abd running
I would recommend taking a pair of cancellation noise headphones...
Fantastic library! Lots if fun activities for pre-schoolers.

name: Newtown Library
address: 8-10 Brown St, Newtown NSW 2042, Australia
formatted_address: 8-10 Brown St, Newtown NSW 2042, Australia

reviews.text:
Long time Newtown local, went for the first time today because I don’t have Microsoft apps on my computer (the programs are hundreds of dollars to buy, and I only needed them for a specific project!). The library is a beautiful space, open air, AC, surrounded by books, quiet, and respectful people! Used the computers (for free) that I needed, they had plenty so multiple were open. BEST part:
today at the front they put on a free beading station! They welcomed everyone!!! So you could take a break from working (as I did), do some crafts (which let’s be honest, working adults hardly get to do), and end up with an awesome self made project !! All organised and put on by people who are super happy for you to be there and want you to interact  or do
Something non-stressful. 10/10 great workspace, great space to be i, and 12/10 librarians who really make an effort for community! Def will be visiting to support more!

Letro local small library in Newtown!
You can bring electric recycles too(bulbs, cables - only small size as the photo)
Great library with a wide selection of books and a quiet, cozy atmosphere. The staff is friendly and always ready to help. Perfect spot for reading or studying!

I had a pleasant experience there but this library lacks sufficient comfortable chairs for people to sit and read.

The upstairs section is appointed like a prison.

A  beautiful  and   cozy     local   library.    it  has a good section    with    children's    books and     an     activities   corner    for    the   little  ones.     The     staff    are    very     helpful.    There    is    a    free    WiFi.
A    great        community     space    in    the      Inner     West.

name: Stanmore Library
address: Douglas St, Stanmore NSW 2048, Australia
formatted_address: Douglas St, Stanmore NSW 2048, Australia

reviews.text: Beautiful little nook that’s just around the corner from the station (but still quiet). Nice chairs to read, tables to study and the kids corner looks fun!

Rediscovering the library was one of the best things, and this is a gorgeous one. It’s amazing how you can order your books online and are delivered from all the inner west library’s to pick up when they are ready in your queue. It has really got me back into reading and so grateful to be offered this brilliant resource of wonderful books.

It is a super convenient location surrounded by cute little coffee shops and right near the train station too.

The librarians are so lovely, it’s a real treasure in the heart of Stanmore. Highly recommend.
Lovely local library with super helpful staff. A book  club meets there once a month.

cute little library! The person at the counter was super helpful and informative when I got my library card, answered all my questions with patience!! very excited to read my books and come right back for more!
I have visited this library over 20 times, everytime the staff has been incredible. Always friendly and personable. Always helpful. Big thumbs up !

name: SciTech Library
address: Jane Foss Russell Building, 160 City Rd, Darlington NSW 2008, Australia
formatted_address: Jane Foss Russell Building, 160 City Rd, Darlington NSW 2008, Australia

reviews.text:
The SciTech Library at the University of Sydney is one of my favourite places on campus. It provides a comfortable and modern study environment for students in science and technology disciplines. One of its most unique features is the sleep pods, which allow students to take short breaks or naps during long study sessions—a perfect spot to recharge before continuing work. Just upstairs, there is a Subway restaurant, making it easy to grab a quick meal or coffee without leaving the building.

The library is also well-equipped with printers and photocopiers, making document preparation and submission convenient. However, the computers can be a bit slow sometimes—it took nearly a full minute for me to log in during my last visit. Despite that minor inconvenience, the overall atmosphere is excellent: bright, quiet, and surrounded by large windows with views of the green campus outside.

Many students gather here to collaborate or study independently, and the space feels both professional and welcoming. With its combination of study facilities, food options, and resting areas, the SciTech Library is truly an ideal place for learning, relaxing, and connecting with the university community.

Toilets are disgusting, NEVER clean, broken pipes/flushing, full of dirty stickers and graffiti, old sinks that look like for kindergarteners, UGLY colours, DEFINITELY not enough tables and seats, AND NOT enough outlets, terrible opening hours, this library should be 24hrs for students and its not because clearly staff and admin cant be bothered :/  hardly any books have been put online, the catelogue system is bad if you search for a chem textbook it gives you literally NOT what you want then they blame you for not being able to research when UNSW clearly doesn't have this problem and is able to show very clearly textbooks for course units or recommended texts. USYD is really failing its students, particularly its science, engineering, med, architecture, computer science... how can you have a library with all the important books be closed on weekends when people study AND  not open 24hrs.

Nice place to study for USYD and other students. Short walk from Redfern Station. More an Undergraduate students library compared to the Fisher. Can find space to chat, or space to study. Can occasionally be loud. Can get packed during exam period. Cool modern style to the interior. Spare rooms for different needs. You can book a desk, though usually people don’t bother, they just sit wherever. My second home

Way too warm, seems a bit dirty. Especially the toilets. Not enough power points to charge laptops.

A bit cramped and it’s clear books are not the main attraction but the study space itself; when you need to disturb people from studying by walking past and possibly asking them to move to access a book you know the design is a bit of an issue.

Some areas of the library are also not very clear as to the functionality (the glass windowed spaces), are they for staff use or students use? Could be made more clear

My favourite library in USyd. Although it is hard to find a seat here, that is no different to the other libraries on campus. This place seems a bit more relaxed, compared to the other libraries. It's quiet but you can also find spaces to talk to friends here if you need.

name: Buddhist Library
address: 90-92 Church St, Camperdown NSW 2050, Australia
formatted_address: 90-92 Church St, Camperdown NSW 2050, Australia

reviews.text: Good place 😁 😃

Hi! I just saw your response to my review which appears to have been done 3 months ago where I gave 3 stars. Complete typo!! I love this place!
I’ve been attending yoga on Saturdays and sanga on Wednesday nights. Couldn’t be happier. Glad I found this place!
Great resource for the Buddhist community and also a decent venue to hire for community events. I did mindfulness course there and have since visited to browse the books. Friendly staff.
Beautiful place, peaceful and calm energy, with some great yoga and meditation classes and talks by monastics and psychologists and other practicing teachers
Peacefully place and wonderful staffs

name: The Women's Library
address: 8-10 Brown St, Newtown NSW 2042, Australia
formatted_address: 8-10 Brown St, Newtown NSW 2042, Australia

reviews.text: What a gorgeous spot. I loved browsing through the collection of books and also the events calendar looks robust- looking fwd to trying out a book club w the library :) well done!!
This is a really excellent resource. It’s a lovely space that is used by various women’s groups as well as a quiet place to read or study. The volunteers are lovely too!
What a great spot! Highly recommend if youre in newtown. Staff is friendly and they had a good range of books.

How many feminists does it take to change a light bulb? One, and it's not funny!
Anyway, here's an interesting hypothetical. Would a woman in a burqa be allowed to borrow from TWL? Such a woman reinforces the patriarchal paradigm, so, probably not. Therefore, TWL is merely a space for privileged, white, bourgeoisie women, just as Simone de Beauvoir's, The Second Sex concerns itself with white, bourgeoisie women.
Very nice Library. I have been for workshops with DecoFem and always is a marvellous experience.

name: Street library
address: Phillip St, Newtown NSW 2042, Australia
formatted_address: Phillip St, Newtown NSW 2042, Australia

reviews.text: It’s just a box with lots of books, you can’t really sit down like real library, I guess that’s why it’s called street library, it’s more like street box with books

wonderous place, filled with magic. I expereinse great joy when take books from it. I never give anyrhing back. I found a book about cat yoga there and my life has changed for the better. lots of love. i am literater.
A bit run down box but one of the most active street library's I've seen.  Always a good selection of classics, literary and popular fiction. If it's empty just cone back later and it won't be.
A sad and broken street library

name: Glebe Library
address: 186 Glebe Point Rd, Glebe NSW 2037, Australia
formatted_address: 186 Glebe Point Rd, Glebe NSW 2037, Australia

reviews.text:
The librarians there are really kind and friendly. The building may not be as modern or glamorous as its counterparts, but the people and atmosphere make it the best library to hang out and borrow books.

Shout out to the librarians on duty today!

Libraries are an integral part of any and every community and neighborhood in all Sydney suburbs. Some libraries are nothing more than an afterthought, but not the Glebe Library. Its location in the centre of Glebe makes it a natural magnet for people who live in the area. It doesn't just have books, but also computers, copiers and a range of services for those who need to borrow a book or work on a document. The Glebe Library is also situated in a beautiful and leafy property that add to the charm of Glebe.

Glebe Library is such a peaceful and welcoming place. The staff are always friendly, helpful, and genuinely love what they do. The space is bright, quiet, and beautifully organized — perfect for reading, studying, or just relaxing with a good book.

Glebe/Newtown etc district libraries- their content is sub-optimal.  I can locate books easier and borrow from Victorian, QLD & North Shore & Eastern suburbs libraries better than City of Sydney content - Im unsure what they spend their money on - one copy across 4 locations???  not generous or welcoming to readers.   Please consider expanding your library content.  Glebe library with the Service Centre is the noisiest library Ive ever been in and I visit different libraries on my travels once a week.  A very uninspiring library - what a shame.

Such a friendly staff and helpful. Good place and clear. Wonderful environment and full service. I applied the member on online and went to the library to proof my identical and get my membership card. They have plenty of books and also provide technology to help their members and staff work easier. I am very impressed.

name: Herbert Smith Freehills Law Library - The University of Sydney Library
address: Sydney Law School Eastern Avenue Camperdown NSW, Australia, Sydney NSW 2050, Australia
formatted_address: Sydney Law School Eastern Avenue Camperdown NSW, Australia, Sydney NSW 2050, Australia

reviews.text: Poorly managed. Bathrooms are always messy and disgusting. Staff (white/female) will take photos of random students studying in the library. Not enough seating to accomodate students
One of my favourite libraries to study at. And opens 24 hours perfect for exam time!
One of USYD's best libraries. Clean, spacious, quiet and a beautiful setting.
Very quiet library, very easy to focus on your work, hard to find a seat sometimes.
The best and coziest library at Sydney Uni. I spent most of my uni life here 😁❤️

name: Street Library
address: 9 Cecily St, Lilyfield NSW 2040, Australia
formatted_address: 9 Cecily St, Lilyfield NSW 2040, Australia

reviews.text: Nice full street library

name: Haberfield Library
address: 78 Dalhousie St, Haberfield NSW 2045, Australia
formatted_address: 78 Dalhousie St, Haberfield NSW 2045, Australia

reviews.text:
We've had the pleasure of bringing our softplay setup and balloon backdrop on various occasions to the Community Center (Michael Maher room) which is directly connected to the library.

The space is beautiful and intimate, but I'd only call out the extreme parking challenges - there's absolutely no parking nearby unless you're lucky, so it was a bit of a mission for us to unload, set up, and load back at the end of the party.

Nevertheless, venue itself is nice and spacing is perfect for an intimate party or celebration!

Thanks to Kim and all the guys at haberfield library. They are all efficient knowlegable and professional in the way they handle customers and all friendly to boot. If your after a quiet relaxed afternoon this is the place to be. Thanks again  Justyn

I visited with my 3 year old for Storytime. We sang songs and read books in a group. The kids had a good time! The library itself is small but very neat and tidy and quite spacious, with a nice little patch of grass for reading outdoors.

Haberfield library was one of my favourite places to visit with it's friendly staff, good selection of books, interesting internal character with comfortable welcoming areas to read, study and use computers, kids running inside after school to find books to read and borrow, they even had a community seed swap box, all before the major renovations occured.
I was excited and expected a big improvements but on visiting the 'new' library I disappointed to find it modern cold and sterile much like a mausoleum.  Bland white walls and dark timber floors and shelves which were lined orderly like pews.
Lost was the warmth, fun and character the old library had to be replaced with modern blandness.
I felt was nothing welcoming or inviting to encourage the discovery of new books to read, and was surprised that the only thing which stood out amongst the inane shelves was the high number of rainbow stickers on the books spine for the LGBT readers.
The study and reading area is at the entrance separated from the actual library which makes no sense.
Sadly the seed swap box is no longer there.

For study purposes and other activities it is great. However I found the seats at the front of the library (the study section) to be extremely high. Students who like to study here would find it a work to crunch over the table from the height of the seats. It would be great if there would be  comfy and suitable chairs when studying.
